headline: French High Council on Climate warns of urgent need to 'scale up' climate policies
subhead: As France endures its third heatwave in under two months, the independent climate watchdog is condemning the slowdown in decarbonization and the rollback of environmental policies, reiterating that France is not ready for climate change.

headline: France fires past Morocco into World Cup semi-finals
Burnham on track to become next UK PM as hundreds of Labour MPs back leadership bid
'Imperial obsession and reckless escalation: Vladimir Putin has never appeared so trapped by his fantasies'
subhead: Defeated in his attempt to subjugate Ukraine by capturing Kyiv in early 2022 and unable, four years later, to conquer all the Ukrainian plains bordering Russia's western border, the Russian leader has taken revenge on civilian populations, says Le Monde columnist Alain Frachon.

headline: 'In trying to avoid accusations of exaggerating, the IPCC has instead underestimated the severity of our summers'
subhead: Long accused of alarmism by the far right, the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC) has, in fact, been too cautious, underestimating the speed of change and its impacts, says historian Jean-Baptiste Fressoz.

headline: Kanye West, banned from most European countries, receives €4 million grant to perform in Albania
subhead: Barred from entering or performing due to antisemitic and neo-Nazi statements, the American rapper is set to take the stage on July 11 in Tirana. Many protesters are accusing Prime Minister Edi Rama of using the event to serve his own interests.

headline: French AI defense chief: 'Our challenge is to confront the malicious uses of AI without betraying our values'
subhead: Innovation in military applications of AI technology is part of a power struggle in which US companies try to prevent the emergence of international competitors, says Bertrand Rondepierre, director of the ministerial agency for defense AI.

Does the decline in reading and the rise of screens signal the end of the democratic era?
subhead: While concerns about the weakening of the mind in the face of new technologies are nothing new, the digital revolution and artificial intelligence now point to a possible end of book civilization.

headline: EWC 2026 in Paris: Behind the scenes of the controversial partnership between France and Saudi Arabia in esports
subhead: By hosting this Saudi video game tournament in Paris, with an opening ceremony on Wednesday, July 8, featuring a concert by Aya Nakamura, the Elysée is endorsing the kingdom's dominance in the sector – but not without facing criticism from the French esports community.

headline: The day Marine Le Pen took back control of her fate, despite a conviction
subhead: After being sentenced to one year of house arrest, the French far right's leader found an opening back into the presidential race.

headline: Populists' temptation of impunity
